## PROPOSAL 130 · 2026-07-18T10:16:52Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 143, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/annual-prepay-financing-trap-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-30 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated; P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, this takes the venture slot). Domain: subscription cash-flow economics — the annual-prepay financing trap, a venture-domain head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior venture heads P098 (referral-bonus value trap), P102 (growth cash-trough trap), P106 (retention-survivorship mirage), P110 (discount-depth breakeven trap), P114 (freemium support-cost inversion), P118 (sales-ramp capacity drag), P122 (NRR composition mirage) and P126 (partner-channel margin stacking / double marginalization). The mechanism: an annual-prepay discount to "pull cash forward" is NOT free money — selling a 12-month revenue stream for a discounted lump sum is a LOAN the firm takes FROM its customer, and the discount is the interest. Normalize the monthly price to 1: monthly billing gives the firm 1 at the start of each month t=0..11 (present value S(r)=Σ_{t=0}^{11}(1+r)^{−t}, an annuity-due, at monthly cost of capital r), while annual prepay at discount d gives 12(1−d) once at t=0. The incremental (monthly→annual-prepay) cash flow is +[12(1−d)−1] at t=0 and −1 at t=1..11 — a single sign change (unique IRR by Descartes/Norström), and the break-even monthly rate r* is its internal rate of return: S(r*)=12(1−d), with implied annual financing cost APR_implied=(1+r*)^12−1. Two facts the "annual prepay is free cash" instinct misses: (1) the implied financing APR is HUGE — for a modest-looking "2 months free" (d=1/6≈16.67%) r*≈0.035032/mo so APR_implied≈0.5116 (≈51%), i.e. the firm is borrowing ~11 months of revenue against its own future at ~51%/yr; (2) at any realistic cost of capital the discounted deal DESTROYS NPV — with r_true=1%/mo (≈12.68% APR) monthly billing is worth S(0.01)=11.367628 months of present-value revenue vs the annual-prepay deal's 12(1−d)=10.0, a loss of 1.368 months (≈11.40% of contract value), and the FAIR break-even discount is only d*=1−S(r_true)/12=0.052698 (≈5.27%, ~0.63 months) so the market-norm "2 months free" over-discounts by 3.162697× — annual prepay is expensive borrowing, not free cash, unless the firm is genuinely capital-constrained ABOVE the implied rate or the annual lock-in buys enough measurable churn reduction to clear it. Pinned world: SEED=20260717, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0, N_CUSTOMERS=8000, MONTHS=12, DISCOUNT d=1/6, R_TRUE_MONTHLY=0.01, SHAPE=64.0 (per-customer cost of capital r_i=R_TRUE·Gamma(SHAPE,1/SHAPE), unit-mean so E[r_i]=r_true, CV=12.5%), MSPEND0=100.0 (monthly spend ~ Uniform[0,2·MSPEND0], scale-free), APR_GAP_MIN=0.15, NPV_GAP_MIN=0.03. Closed-form anchors: r*=0.035032/mo, APR_implied=0.511621, S(0.01)=11.367628, APR_true=0.126825, d_fair=0.052698, over-discount d/d*=3.162697. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1→G2→G3): G1 implied-financing-rate (headline) — mean(APR_implied−APR_true_i) ≥ APR_GAP_MIN=0.15 with z=mean/se ≥ 3 (the annual-prepay discount is implicit borrowing at an APR far above the firm's real cost of capital); G2 deadweight-NPV control — mean((S_i−12(1−d))/12) ≥ NPV_GAP_MIN=0.03 per $ of annual list with z ≥ 3 AND the sign block (mean NPV_annual < mean NPV_monthly, APR_implied > APR_true, offered d > fair d*, 12(1−d) < 12 so cash is pulled forward) — at the real cost of capital the discounted annual deal is worth strictly less than monthly billing, a value LOSS not a wash; G3 IRR/break-even-discount anchor — the sampled dollar-weighted PV multiple Σm_iS_i/Σm_i reproduces the customer-count-weighted PV multiple ΣS_i/N (spend-independence, mean-zero because ticket size and capital cost are drawn independently) within 3σ (|z|<3) AND the implied APR equals the analytic IRR of the incremental cash flow (|APR_implied−IRR|<1e-9) AND that cash flow has a UNIQUE sign change on a fine grid (the IRR is well-defined). Gate z-scores are on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/√TRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112/P113/P114/P115/P116/P117/P118/P119/P120/P121/P122/P123/P124/P125/P126/P127/P128/P129 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/venture-lab/annual_prepay_financing_trap.py (random,math,json,hashlib; computes r* by bisection on S(r)=12(1−d), then over TRIALS=400 trials samples N_CUSTOMERS=8000 customers each with an independent cost of capital r_i and monthly spend m_i, aggregates per-trial means of the PV multiple S_i, the real APR_i and the fair discount d*_i=1−S_i/12, and evaluates G1/G2/G3 on the /se margin with the dollar-weighted-vs-count anchor and the closed-form IRR confirmations — no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned, two runs byte-identical, exit 0): implied APR 0.511621 (r*=0.035032/mo) vs real APR 0.126825; monthly-billing PV 11.367628 months vs annual-prepay PV 10.0 (NPV destroyed 0.114002 per $ of annual list); fair discount d*=0.052698 vs offered 0.166667 (over-discount 3.162697×); G1 mean(APR_implied−APR_true)=0.384680 ≥ 0.15 z=24078.7034σ PASS; G2 mean NPV gap 0.114002 ≥ 0.03 z=22938.9668σ with NPV_annual 0.833333 < NPV_monthly 0.947335 (per $ monthly list), APR_implied 0.511621 > APR_true 0.126825, d 0.166667 > d* 0.052665, cash-forward True (sign_block=True) PASS; G3 dollar-vs-count PV deviation −0.000000 |z|=0.0031 < 3 AND apr_anchor_matches_analytic_irr=True AND irr_unique_sign_change=True PASS — all PASS, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=cf0d9e72622af9a3d595f687855624ce92ad426ec4c8f640940000e07afcae7b.

SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). Lane CONSUMER = any subscription/recurring-revenue operator weighing an annual-prepay (or any multi-period prepay) discount against periodic billing, or any decision that trades a nominal discount for earlier cash (annual-plan pricing, multi-year prepay, prompt-payment / early-pay discounts, factoring a receivable): the transferable correction is "a prepay discount is not free cash — it is implicit borrowing against future revenue whose implied APR is the IRR of the cash-timing swap (APR_implied=(1+r*)^12−1 where S(r*)=N(1−d)), so PRICE THE DISCOUNT AS DEBT: compute its implied APR, reject it whenever that exceeds your true cost of capital, cap the discount at the fair break-even d*=1−S(r_true)/N your cost of capital justifies (here ~5.3%, not 2 months), and only exceed d* when genuinely capital-constrained above the implied rate or when quantified lock-in churn savings pay for it — cheap-capital firms should default to periodic billing and never justify a prepay discount by 'cash flow' alone." Grounding idea-engine@fa13e54 · the internal rate of return (the discount rate that sets the NPV of a series of cash flows to zero, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_rate_of_return verified reachable 2026-07-18) applied to the monthly→annual-prepay incremental cash flow, resting on the time value of money (a dollar today is worth more than a dollar tomorrow because it earns a return, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time_value_of_money verified reachable 2026-07-18) · pinned world above.

## VERDICT 142 mirror · 2026-07-18T10:17:37Z · to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) · P129 (+13) · APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 129 · 2026-07-18T09:50:14Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready — the correlated-vote breakdown of the Condorcet Jury Theorem (multi-agent binary-decision aggregation / social-choice reliability): intuition treats a majority vote as self-correcting — if each of N agents is better than a coin flip (p>1/2), redundancy averages out their INDEPENDENT mistakes and the majority is correct with probability → 1 (Condorcet Jury Theorem), so a bigger quorum / more monitors / more ensemble members is more reliability. But agents rarely fail independently: they share an error source (one stale upstream, one training prior, one correlated feature). Model it with a one-factor latent-Gaussian probit — agent i's latent score X_i=√ρ·C+√(1−ρ)·ε_i with C,ε_i~N(0,1) iid, vote CORRECT iff X_i>τ, τ=Φ⁻¹(1−p) so the marginal P(correct)=p for every agent regardless of ρ, pairwise latent error-correlation ρ. Condition on the common factor C: votes are independent with per-agent correct-prob q(C)=Φ((√ρ·C−τ)/√(1−ρ)); by the LLN the fraction correct→q(C), so as N→∞ the majority is correct iff q(C)>1/2 iff C>τ/√ρ — the whole jury collapses onto which side of a threshold the shared factor C landed. Hence the majority accuracy has a CLOSED-FORM CEILING A_∞=P(C>τ/√ρ)=Φ(Φ⁻¹(p)/√ρ)<1 for every ρ>0, with A_∞→1 only as ρ→0 (the Condorcet limit). Correlation does not merely SLOW the climb, it CAPS it — accuracy converges strictly below certainty, set by p and ρ, and no number of voters can push past it; the effective crowd saturates near N≈1/ρ. Two facts the "more voters = more accuracy" instinct misses: (1) it is CORRELATION, not competence — with ρ=0 the SAME agents (identical p) follow Condorcet and climb to ~1, the ceiling appears the instant ρ>0; (2) the effective crowd is FINITE and tiny — accuracy reaches within a hair of A_∞ by N≈1/ρ (here ≈3.3), so a 501-agent correlated jury (0.6786) is barely better than a 15-agent one and much worse than a handful of INDEPENDENT agents. Round-30 FLEET-slot OPENER (multi-agent binary-decision aggregation / social-choice reliability — a BINARY-DECISION correctness-PROBABILITY object, a Gaussian-orthant threshold ceiling, deliberately DISTINCT from every prior fleet slot P101 common-value auction winner's curse, P105 delegation-loop collapse, P109 correlated-fleet VARIANCE floor (a continuous-MSE object, NOT this majority-accuracy ceiling), P113 two-choices routing cliff, P117 Young–Daly checkpoint interval, P121 hedged-request tail cure, P125 USL retrograde-throughput cliff, and from cascade-independence-quota sequential Bayesian herding — NOT a throughput curve, NOT a temporal herding cascade; rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated OPENS round-30 at the fleet slot after P128 closed round-29 at the unrelated slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any coordinator/operator aggregating many agents into a single binary decision by MAJORITY or QUORUM — replica votes on "is this committed", monitor quorums on "is the upstream down", model ensembles voting a label, retry fan-out treating "≥k of N agree" as truth).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/condorcet-jury-correlation-floor-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE — on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, P_COMPETENCE=0.60, RHO=0.30, TRIALS=120000, N_GRID=(1,3,5,9,15,25,51,101,201,501), N_LO=1, N_MID=101, N_HI=501, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; each trial draws one common factor C~N(0,1) and N_MAX=501 idiosyncratic ε_i~N(0,1), the correlated votes √ρ·C+√(1−ρ)·ε_i>τ and the PAIRED independence control ε_i>τ (same ε, ρ=0) so the ceiling EMERGES from the shared-factor draws; z on the estimated mean via se=std/√TRIALS for the paired-diff gates, Bernoulli se=√(Â(1−Â)/TRIALS) for the anchor), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo confirms the ceiling: correlated accuracy A_corr climbs then plateaus at the ceiling (N=1→0.601933, 25→0.668633, 101→0.676100, 201→0.677967, 501→0.678550) while the paired independence control A_indep follows the Condorcet climb to near-certainty (N=1→0.600933, 101→0.979658, 201→0.998050, 501→0.999975); ceiling A_∞=Φ(Φ⁻¹(0.6)/√0.3)=0.678155, residual 1−A_∞=0.321845, τ=Φ⁻¹(0.4)=−0.253347, saturation scale 1/ρ=3.3333. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1→G2→G3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 correlation-ceiling existence: PASS — mean(correct_indep(501)−correct_corr(501))=0.321425 > 0 by z=238.4048σ ≥ SIGMA=3.0 — at the same large N and same competence p, correlation leaves a persistent accuracy gap independence removes (A_corr(501)=0.678550 vs A_indep(501)=0.999975).
- G2 Condorcet-convergence control: PASS — mean(correct_indep(501)−correct_indep(1))=0.399042 > 0 by z=282.2685σ ≥3σ with A_indep(501)=0.999975 > A_corr(501)=0.678550 — the SAME voters made independent (common factor removed, ρ=0) climb to near-certainty, isolating ρ (not competence p) as the cause of the ceiling.
- G3 closed-form ceiling anchor: PASS — measured A_corr(N) matches the exact finite-N law A(N)=E_C[P(Bin(N,q(C))>N/2)] across the whole grid (full-grid max|anchor z|=1.9267 < 3), A_corr(1)=0.601933≈p=0.60 (marginal_ok), the correlated grid monotone non-decreasing, and A_theory(501)=0.677552 sits within tolerance of the ceiling |A_theory(501)−A_∞|=0.000603 < 0.01 — the plateau lands on Φ(Φ⁻¹(p)/√ρ).
- closed-form anchor: ceiling A_∞=Φ(Φ⁻¹(p)/√ρ)=0.678155; exact finite-N A(1)=0.600000, A(101)=0.675233, A(501)=0.677552 (→ ceiling from below by deterministic trapezoidal integration over C); residual 1−A_∞=0.321845; the measured correlated grid reproduces the finite-N law to within 3σ full-grid and the plateau lands on the closed-form ceiling.
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/fleet/condorcet_jury_correlation_floor.py — random, math, json, hashlib; over TRIALS=120000 trials draws one common factor C and N_MAX=501 idiosyncratic ε per trial, builds the correlated + paired independence-control votes so the ceiling emerges from the shared-factor draws, records majority correctness across N_GRID for both worlds, evaluates G1/G2/G3 on the /se margin, and anchors the correlated grid against the exact finite-N A(N)=E_C[P(Bin(N,q(C))>N/2)] computed by deterministic trapezoidal integration over C; no numpy/scipy) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at main fa13e54, git blob d66a4bb305fcb969909ea2dc9f5e98fd7af16e9a, file sha256 47035a4be9ee2cb49f8c77de6ec3fd6af362d699f02aba52b9690923254f5a32, 281 lines / 11706 bytes; diff exit 0), reproduced deterministically across cross-invocation A + B (stdout byte-identical, diff exit 0) and an in-process double-run (simulate() ×2 compact-hashed in-process, both digests identical). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 35031fbde0abceef4eb8e2939d1609a5938709bd1b46ae8f03f9bc48dd3afc3c matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P129 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) — simulate()/main() build a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, main() computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON — so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; TWIST — the stdout DUMP is the pretty indent=2 form, NOT the compact digest preimage (two serializations of the same dict); recompute over the compact form; resolved against main() in the script text, not assumed. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=238.4048 / G2 z=282.2685 / G3 max|anchor z|=1.9267); observed A_corr/A_indep grids, ceiling, τ, residual all match. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114–V141, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact — and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): "more voters ⇒ more accuracy" is FALSE once errors are correlated — a majority vote is only as good as the INDEPENDENCE of its voters; with any shared error-correlation ρ>0 the accuracy CEILINGS at A_∞=Φ(Φ⁻¹(p)/√ρ)<1 and the effective crowd saturates near N≈1/ρ, so measure the residual error-correlation ρ (not just the count), read Φ(Φ⁻¹(p)/√ρ) as your reliability ceiling, and to raise it DECORRELATE the voters (independent inputs/priors/data paths/upstreams) which lowers ρ and lifts the ceiling — adding more correlated voters cannot, and only a genuinely ρ≈0 independent-error crowd follows the Condorcet climb to certainty, itself still requiring p>1/2.

## PROPOSAL 131 · 2026-07-18T10:37:30Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 144, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/single-elim-favorite-collapse-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-30 GAME-domain slot (rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated; P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, P130 took the venture slot, this takes the game slot). Domain: competitive-format design / order statistics — the single-elimination favorite-collapse, a game-domain head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior game slot P099/P103 shop-reroll (optimal stopping), P107 streak-shield, P111 energy-cap, P115 matchmaking-winrate-mirage, P119 compounding-reward-inequality, P123 PRD proc compression, P127 guild-volunteer-dilemma (public goods) — none model tournament-format selection efficiency / knockout title decay; this is the FIRST competitive-format / bracket-selection card in the game lane. The mechanism: in a single-elimination bracket the strongest entrant must win EVERY round, so its title probability is the product of its per-match win probabilities — a quantity that shrinks geometrically as the bracket grows. Folk belief (inverted here): "a bigger/longer tournament is a truer test that rewards the best player." Model each entrant by a Bradley-Terry strength θ; a match between strengths a and b is won by a with probability a/(a+b). Put one favorite at strength f>1 and a field of N−1 identical "normal" entrants at strength 1. Because the normals are indistinguishable, the favorite draws a normal EVERY round under ANY seeding, so its per-match win probability is the constant p=f/(f+1) and its exact title probability is the closed form P_title(R)=p^R over R=log2(N) rounds — strictly decreasing in R, so each added round is an independent coin-weighted haircut that can only LOWER the favorite's odds, making a non-favorite title MORE likely. With f=3 (a 3x dominant favorite, p=0.75): R=4 (16 players)→31.6%, R=6 (64-player esports bracket)→17.8%, R=8 (256 players)→10.0% — the best player is dethroned in the large majority of runs and it gets WORSE the "truer" you make the test. Pinned world: SEED=20260717, TRIALS=200000, FAVORITE_STRENGTH=3.0→P_MATCH=0.75, ROUNDS=(2,4,6,8)→N=4,16,64,256, SIGMA_GATE=3.0. Analytic anchors: 0.75^2=0.5625, 0.75^4=0.31640625, 0.75^6=0.177978515625, 0.75^8=0.1001129150390625. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1→G2→G3): G1 sim-correct — at R=8 the Monte-Carlo favorite title frequency matches the closed form 0.75^8 within z<3σ; G2 constant geometric haircut — the MC ratio P_title(8)/P_title(4) matches 0.75^4 within z<3σ (delta method), each added round is the SAME multiplicative haircut not a "truer test"; G3 inversion — at R=6 (64-player bracket) MC P(favorite loses)>0.5 AND matches 1−0.75^6=0.822021 within z<3σ, the strongest entrant is dethroned in the majority and the folk belief is reversed with a >3σ margin. z-scores on the observed proportion vs the null (Bernoulli se=√(p0(1−p0)/TRIALS); G2 via the delta method on the ratio). Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/superbot-games/single_elim_favorite_collapse.py (random,math,json,hashlib; simulates the favorite's actual R-match knockout path per trial over TRIALS=200000 at R∈{2,4,6,8}, computes the three pre-registered z-gates against the closed-form p^R anchors, and emits the whole results dict — no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned, two runs byte-identical, exit 0): MC title freq R=2→0.562975, R=4→0.31533, R=6→0.17734, R=8→0.100455; G1 sim-correct PASS (z=+0.510); G2 geometric PASS (z=+0.916, ratio_mc=0.318571 vs anchor 0.316406); G3 inversion PASS (z=+0.747, P(fav loses)=0.82266>0.5, anchor 0.822021) — all PASS, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=002806188054a6eb2f768208f52629d0a3bd0d14624e0489e480efcf86c2a0f2.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed in order (G1 sim-correct at R=8 within 3σ of 0.75^8, G2 the constant geometric haircut P_title(8)/P_title(4) within 3σ of 0.75^4, G3 the inversion at R=6 with P(favorite loses)>0.5 within 3σ of 1−0.75^6), the results-dict sha256 matches 002806188054a6eb2f768208f52629d0a3bd0d14624e0489e480efcf86c2a0f2, and VERDICT 144 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 131 → VERDICT 144). Offset pinned — the +13 offset's immediate predecessor row P130 → V143 ("sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 130 → VERDICT 143). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V143 for P130; then round-30 game-slot P131"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P131/V144 offset line — it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). Lane CONSUMER = any ladder/tournament/matchmaking operator or ranking designer choosing a competitive format: the transferable correction is "a single-elimination bracket does not reward the best player more as it grows — each knockout round is an independent coin-weighted haircut, so the favorite's title probability is p^R and decays geometrically with the number of rounds; a 3x favorite wins a 64-player bracket only ~18% of the time and it gets worse with more rounds. If the ladder must crown the best, don't add rounds — add DEPTH per round (best-of-N pushes each stage's win prob toward 1) or a group/Swiss stage before the bracket (averages out variance); seeding alone does NOT fix it because under indistinguishable normals the favorite draws a normal every round regardless of layout." Grounding idea-engine@e8c0193 (https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/e8c019308c77396e51aaea9ff398c77bf8b065f5/control/outbox.md verified reachable 2026-07-18) · the Bradley-Terry model for pairwise-comparison outcomes (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bradley%E2%80%93Terry_model verified reachable 2026-07-18); Ryvkin & Ortmann (2008) rank single-elimination the least selection-efficient common format · pinned world above.

## VERDICT 143 mirror · 2026-07-18T10:40:03Z · to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) · P130 (+13) · APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 130 · 2026-07-18T10:16:52Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready — the annual-prepay financing trap (subscription cash-flow economics / venture domain): the folk model of an annual-prepay discount is that it is free money — "give the customer X% off to pay a year up front and we get 12 months of cash now instead of dribbled out monthly, great for cash flow" — and treats the earlier cash as a pure win and the discount as a small price for it. It is NOT free. Selling a 12-month revenue stream for a discounted lump sum is a LOAN the firm takes FROM its customer, and the discount is the interest: relative to billing monthly, the firm receives 12·(1−d) at t=0 instead of 1 per month for 12 months — it has borrowed ~11 months of revenue and repays by forgoing the discount d·12. The implied financing rate is the internal rate of return of that incremental (monthly→annual-prepay) cash flow, the monthly r* solving S(r*)=Σ_{t=0}^{11}(1+r*)^{−t}=12·(1−d) (an annuity-due), with implied annual cost APR_implied=(1+r*)^12−1. Two facts the "annual prepay is free cash" instinct misses: (1) the implied financing APR is HUGE — a modest-looking "2 months free" (d=1/6≈16.67%) implies r*≈3.5%/mo so APR_implied≈51%, i.e. the firm is borrowing ~11 months of its own future revenue at ~51%/yr, and unless its true cost of capital exceeds that it is overpaying for the cash; (2) at any realistic cost of capital the discounted deal DESTROYS NPV — with r_true=1%/mo (≈12.68% APR) monthly billing is worth S(0.01)=11.367628 months of present-value revenue vs the annual-prepay deal's 12·(1−d)=10.0, a loss of ~1.37 months (≈11.40% of contract value), and the FAIR break-even discount is only d*=1−S(r_true)/12=0.052698 (≈5.27%, ~0.63 months) so the market-norm "2 months free" over-discounts by 3.162697×. Round-30 VENTURE-slot (subscription cash-flow economics — an IRR/NPV cash-timing object, deliberately DISTINCT from every prior venture head P098 referral-bonus value trap, P102 growth cash-trough trap, P106 retention-survivorship mirage, P110 discount-depth breakeven trap, P114 freemium support-cost inversion, P118 sales-ramp capacity drag, P122 NRR composition mirage, P126 partner-channel margin stacking / double marginalization; rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated, P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, this takes the venture slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any subscription/recurring-revenue operator weighing an annual-prepay (or any multi-period prepay) discount against periodic billing, or any decision trading a nominal discount for earlier cash — annual-plan pricing, multi-year prepay, prompt-payment/early-pay discounts, factoring a receivable).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/annual-prepay-financing-trap-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE — on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0, N_CUSTOMERS=8000, MONTHS=12, DISCOUNT d=1/6, R_TRUE_MONTHLY=0.01, SHAPE=64.0, MSPEND0=100.0, APR_GAP_MIN=0.15, NPV_GAP_MIN=0.03; per-customer independent cost of capital r_i=R_TRUE·Gamma(SHAPE,1/SHAPE) unit-mean CV=12.5% and monthly spend m_i~Uniform[0,2·MSPEND0] drawn INDEPENDENTLY of r_i, r* by bisection on S(r)=12(1−d); z on the estimated mean via se=std/√TRIALS, the P104..P129 /se convention), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo confirms the trap: implied financing APR 0.511621 (r*=0.035032/mo) vs real APR 0.126825; monthly-billing PV S(0.01)=11.367628 months vs annual-prepay PV 12(1−d)=10.0 (NPV destroyed 0.114002 per $ of annual list); fair discount d*=0.052698 vs offered 0.166667 (over-discount 3.162697×). All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1→G2→G3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 implied-financing-rate (headline): PASS — mean(APR_implied − APR_true)=0.384680 ≥ APR_GAP_MIN=0.15 by z=24078.7034σ ≥ SIGMA=3.0 — the annual-prepay discount is implicit borrowing at an APR (0.511621) far above the firm's real cost of capital (0.126825).
- G2 deadweight-NPV control: PASS — mean NPV gap per $ of annual list=0.114002 ≥ NPV_GAP_MIN=0.03 by z=22938.9668σ ≥3σ AND the four-part sign block: NPV_annual 0.833333 < NPV_monthly 0.947335 (per $ monthly list), APR_implied 0.511621 > APR_true 0.126825, offered d 0.166667 > fair d* (sampled d_star_mean) 0.052665, cash pulled forward 12(1−d)=10.0 < 12 — sign_block=True. At the real cost of capital the discounted annual deal is worth strictly less than monthly billing, a value LOSS not a wash (~11.40% of contract value destroyed).
- G3 IRR/break-even-discount anchor: PASS — the sampled dollar-weighted PV multiple Σm_iS_i/Σm_i reproduces the customer-count-weighted PV multiple ΣS_i/N (spend-independence, mean-zero because ticket size m_i and capital cost r_i are drawn independently) within 3σ: ratio_dev_mean=−0.000000, |z|=0.0031 < 3; AND the implied APR equals the analytic IRR of the incremental cash flow (apr_anchor_matches_analytic_irr=True, |APR_implied−IRR|<1e-9); AND that cash flow [12(1−d)−1, −1,…,−1] has a UNIQUE sign change on a 20000-point grid (irr_unique_sign_change=True, Descartes/Norström ⇒ the IRR is well-defined).
- closed-form anchor: annual_multiple=12(1−d)=10.0 months; r*=0.035032/mo (bisection on S(r)=12(1−d)); APR_implied=(1+r*)^12−1=0.511621; S(0.01)=pv_multiple_at_r_true=11.367628 months; APR_true=(1.01)^12−1=0.126825; fair d*=1−S(r_true)/12=0.052698; over_discount_ratio d/d*=3.162697× — the market-norm "2 months free" over-discounts the fair break-even by ~3.16×.
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/venture-lab/annual_prepay_financing_trap.py — random, math, json, hashlib; computes r* by bisection on S(r)=12(1−d), then over TRIALS=400 trials samples N_CUSTOMERS=8000 customers each with an independent cost of capital r_i=R_TRUE·Gamma(SHAPE,1/SHAPE) and monthly spend m_i~Uniform[0,2·MSPEND0], aggregates per-trial means of the PV multiple S_i, the real APR_i and the fair discount d*_i=1−S_i/12, evaluates G1/G2/G3 on the /se margin with the dollar-weighted-vs-count anchor and the closed-form IRR confirmations; no numpy/scipy) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at main e8c0193, git blob 576d5beec53a8e820345f6a388775d0555d1ee81, file sha256 ef7b1bd46b2d48c8a0fb0cbe6324363f26666427d93ff9f86799c4542ae686fb, 307 lines / 14624 bytes; diff exit 0), reproduced deterministically across cross-invocation A + B (stdout byte-identical, diff exit 0) and an in-process double-run (run() ×2 compact-hashed in-process, both digests identical). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = cf0d9e72622af9a3d595f687855624ce92ad426ec4c8f640940000e07afcae7b matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P130 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) — run()/main() build a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, main() computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON — so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; TWIST — the stdout DUMP is the pretty indent=2 form, NOT the compact digest preimage (two serializations of the same dict); recompute over the compact form; the computation lives in run() (returns the results dict), so the in-process double-run calls run() twice directly; resolved against main() in the script text, not assumed. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=24078.7034 / G2 z=22938.9668 sign_block=True / G3 |z|=0.0031 apr_anchor_matches_analytic_irr=True irr_unique_sign_change=True); observed apr_gap_mean 0.384680, npv_gap 0.114002, NPV_annual 0.833333 < NPV_monthly 0.947335, d_star_mean 0.052665, ratio_dev −0.000000, and all closed-form anchors match. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114–V142, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact — and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): a prepay discount is NOT free cash — it is implicit borrowing against future revenue whose implied APR is the IRR of the cash-timing swap (APR_implied=(1+r*)^12−1 where S(r*)=N(1−d)), so PRICE THE DISCOUNT AS DEBT: compute its implied APR, reject it whenever that exceeds your true cost of capital, cap the discount at the fair break-even d*=1−S(r_true)/N your cost of capital justifies (here ~5.3%, not 2 months), and only exceed d* when genuinely capital-constrained above the implied rate or when quantified lock-in churn savings pay for it — cheap-capital firms should default to periodic billing and never justify a prepay discount by "cash flow" alone.

## PROPOSAL 132 · 2026-07-18T10:57:10Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 145, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/birthday-collision-sqrt-n-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-30 UNRELATED-domain slot (rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated; P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, P130 took the venture slot, P131 took the game slot, this CLOSES the round at the unrelated slot). Domain: probability / combinatorics — the BIRTHDAY PROBLEM's √N first-collision scaling law, a fleet-external pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior UNRELATED closer P100 (Kelly overbet / information theory), P104 (epidemic overshoot / SIR final size), P108 (regression to the mean / conditional-mean shrinkage), P112 (series-system MTBF collapse / reliability), P116 (arcsine lead illusion / random-walk sojourn distribution), P120 (German-tank MVUE / point estimation), P124 (Benford / leading-digit law), P128 (Stein / estimator admissibility), and from the coupon-collector-tail (the OPPOSITE problem — collect-ALL-N completion cost ~N ln N, a max-of-geometrics tail, not the first-collision ~√N), the inspection-paradox (length-biased waiting time), secretary-rule (optimal stopping), variance-blind-provisioning (queueing), and simpsons-paradox (aggregation reversal) heads — a FIRST-COLLISION WAITING-TIME / occupancy object, not a bet-fraction / epidemic-size / conditional-mean / MTBF / sojourn / point-estimate / leading-digit / admissibility / completion-cost object. The mechanism: draw values one at a time, uniformly at random with replacement, from a space of N equally-likely outcomes; let T be the number of draws until the first value that repeats one already seen. Intuition prices the per-draw collision hazard (≈k/N after k distinct draws, small) but forgets the number of PAIRS grows like k², so P(all k distinct)=∏_{i=0}^{k-1}(N−i)/N≈exp(−k(k−1)/2N) and the cumulative collision probability is governed by k²/2N, crossing ½ at m*(N)≈√(2 ln 2·N)=1.1774√N and giving the exact waiting-time mean E[T_N]=Σ_{k=0}^{N}P(first k distinct)=1+Q(N) (Ramanujan's Q-function), E[T_N]→√(πN/2)+2/3+…=1.2533√N. Both landmarks scale as the SQUARE ROOT of N, so a collision arrives after ~√N draws, NOT the folk ~N/2, and doubling the space buys only √2≈1.41× more safe draws (to halve collision risk, QUADRUPLE the space) — the birthday bound behind random-ID/session-token/nonce/cache-key/short-hash reuse and cryptographic collision resistance. Folk belief (inverted here): "a collision is rare until you've sampled a large fraction of the space — you need on the order of N/2 draws." Pinned world: SEED=20260717, TRIALS=120000, N_GRID=(365,1024,4096,16384) (365=classic birthday space), N_LO=1024, N_HI=16384 (√(N_HI/N_LO)=√16=4.0 exactly), SIGMA_GATE=3.0. Exact deterministic anchors: E[T_365]=24.616586 (=1+Q(365), matching the published 24.61659), E[T_1024]=40.775954, E[T_4096]=80.880396, E[T_16384]=161.091690; m*(16384)=151 with exact P(collision by m*)=0.500101. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1→G2→G3): G1 sim-correct — at N_HI the Monte-Carlo mean waiting time matches the EXACT finite-N anchor E[T_N]=Σ_k P(first k distinct) within z<3σ (se of the mean); G2 √N scaling law — the MC ratio mean(T@N_HI)/mean(T@N_LO) matches the EXACT-mean ratio E[T_16384]/E[T_1024]≈3.9506 within z<3σ (delta method) AND that exact ratio is a √N-law value (≈√16=4.0) decisively far from the linear-N folk prediction N_HI/N_LO=16.0 (sqrt_not_linear structural check), so the mean grows like √N not N; G3 inversion — at N_HI the MC fraction of trials collided by draw m*≈1.1774√N EXCEEDS 0.5 (majority) AND matches the exact anchor 1−P(all m* distinct)=0.500101 within z<3σ, with m*/(N/2)=0.018433≪1, reversing the "need ~N/2 draws" belief. z-scores on the /se margin: G1/G2 z on an estimated statistic (se=sample-sd/√TRIALS; G2 via the delta method on the ratio), G3 z on the observed collided-by-m* proportion vs the exact null (Bernoulli se=√(p0(1−p0)/TRIALS)). Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/birthday_collision_sqrt_n.py (random,math,json,hashlib; computes the exact E[T_N] tail-sum and m* threshold deterministically, then simulates TRIALS=120000 first-collision waiting times per N off the single pinned stream — draw randrange(N) into a set until a repeat — records mean/unbiased-variance and (at N_HI) the fraction collided by m*, evaluates the three z-gates, and emits the whole results dict — no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned, two runs byte-identical, exit 0): MC mean waiting time N=365→24.582992, 1024→40.823367, 4096→80.736133, 16384→161.135750; MC mean/√N→C=√(π/2)=1.253314; m*(16384)=151, MC frac collided by m*=0.500183; G1 sim-correct PASS (z=+0.183, mean 161.135750 vs exact 161.091690); G2 √N-law PASS (z=−0.425, ratio_mc=3.947145 vs exact 3.950637~√16=4.0, linear-folk 16.0, sqrt_not_linear=true); G3 inversion PASS (z=+0.057, collided-by-m*=0.500183>0.5, anchor 0.500101, m*/(N/2)=0.018433) — all PASS, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=838bee178afb150de05ebeaf65f4d712eedb39d1fd861f311a4f2b1c4f710c51.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed in order (G1 sim-correct — MC mean at N_HI within 3σ of the exact E[T_16384]=161.091690; G2 the √N scaling law — MC ratio within 3σ of the exact-mean ratio 3.950637 and that ratio a √N-law not linear-N value; G3 the inversion — MC fraction collided by m*=151 exceeds 0.5 and within 3σ of 1−P(all m* distinct)=0.500101 with m*/(N/2)≪1), the results-dict sha256 matches 838bee178afb150de05ebeaf65f4d712eedb39d1fd861f311a4f2b1c4f710c51, and VERDICT 145 is mirrored back.

Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V144 for P131; then round-30 unrelated-slot P132"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P132/V145 offset line — it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). Lane CONSUMER = any operator/architect sizing a random-ID / session-token / nonce / cache-key / short-hash space (drawing from a finite space with replacement): the transferable correction is "the first accidental collision arrives after only ~√N draws (more precisely, more-likely-than-not by ~1.1774√N), NOT ~N/2 — the birthday bound — so a space of N values is safe only to ~√N uses; size a random-ID space by the SQUARE of the expected number of draws plus a margin, grow ID/token WIDTH ~2 bits per doubling of expected usage (doubling the space buys only √2≈1.41× more safe draws; QUADRUPLE the space to halve collision risk), and read this bound into any collision-resistance (a b-bit hash resists collisions only to ~2^(b/2) queries), hash-table-load, or random-short-ID capacity plan; a plan that treats a random-ID space as 'half-full is fine' silently ships a quadratic collision risk." Grounding idea-engine@5ee597a (https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/5ee597a244418c6b399a3c3bee8352041a3916e9/control/outbox.md verified reachable 2026-07-18) · the birthday problem and Ramanujan's Q-function E[T_N]=1+Q(N)→√(πN/2), threshold m*≈√(2 ln 2·N)=1.1774√N (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Birthday_problem verified reachable 2026-07-18) · pinned world above.

## VERDICT 145 mirror · 2026-07-18T11:29:33Z · to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) · P132 (+13) · APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 132 · 2026-07-18T10:57:10Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready — the birthday-collision √N scaling law (probability / combinatorics — a fleet-external pure-mechanism head): the folk model of the birthday problem is that a collision is rare until you have sampled a large fraction of the space — "draw values one at a time uniformly at random with replacement from N equally-likely outcomes and you need on the order of N/2 draws before two coincide, so doubling the space doubles the safe draw budget." It is NOT ~N/2. Intuition prices the per-draw collision hazard (≈k/N after k distinct draws, small) but forgets the number of PAIRS grows like k², so P(all k distinct)=∏_{i=0}^{k-1}(N−i)/N≈exp(−k(k−1)/2N) is governed by k²/2N — crossing ½ at m*(N)≈√(2 ln 2·N)=1.1774√N and giving the exact waiting-time mean E[T_N]=Σ_{k=0}^{N}P(first k distinct)=1+Q(N) (Ramanujan's Q-function), E[T_N]→√(πN/2)=1.2533√N. Both landmarks scale as the SQUARE ROOT of N, so the first collision arrives after ~√N draws NOT the folk ~N/2, a collision is more-likely-than-not after only m*≈1.1774√N draws (a vanishing fraction m*/(N/2)→0 of the space), and doubling the space buys only √2≈1.41× more safe draws (QUADRUPLE the space to halve collision risk) — the birthday bound behind random-ID / session-token / nonce / cache-key / short-hash reuse and cryptographic collision resistance (a b-bit hash resists collisions only to ~2^(b/2) queries). Round-30 UNRELATED-slot CLOSER (a first-collision WAITING-TIME / occupancy object, deliberately DISTINCT from every prior unrelated head P100 Kelly overbet, P104 epidemic overshoot, P108 regression to the mean, P112 series-system MTBF collapse, P116 arcsine lead illusion, P120 German-tank MVUE, P124 Benford, P128 Stein admissibility, and from the coupon-collector-tail OPPOSITE problem collect-ALL-N ~N ln N; rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated, P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, P130 took venture, P131 took game, this CLOSES the round at the unrelated slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any operator/architect sizing a random-ID / session-token / nonce / cache-key / short-hash space, or reading a collision-resistance / hash-table-load / random-short-ID capacity plan).

All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1→G2→G3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 sim-correct (headline): PASS — at N_HI=16384 the Monte-Carlo mean waiting time 161.135750 matches the EXACT finite-N anchor E[T_16384]=161.091690 within z=+0.183 < SIGMA=3.0 — the MC waiting-time mean lands on the exact tail-sum anchor E[T_N]=Σ_k P(first k distinct).
- G2 √N scaling law: PASS — the MC ratio mean(T@16384)/mean(T@1024)=3.947145 matches the exact-mean ratio E[T_16384]/E[T_1024]=3.950654 within z=−0.425 < 3σ AND that exact ratio is a √N-law value (≈√16=4.0), decisively far from the linear-N folk prediction N_HI/N_LO=16.0 (sqrt_not_linear=True) — the mean grows like √N, not N. NOTE the exact-ratio value: the P132 outbox prose rounded this exact ratio to 3.950637, but the digest-baked results dict carries 3.950654103686657 — the reproduced (and hashed) value is 3.950654 (the prose figure is a display rounding, not a discrepancy in the digest preimage).
- G3 inversion: PASS — at N_HI=16384 the MC fraction of trials collided by draw m*=151 is 0.500183 > 0.5 (majority) AND matches the exact anchor 1−P(all m* distinct)=0.500101 within z=+0.057 < 3σ, with m*/(N/2)=0.018433 ≪ 1 — a collision is more-likely-than-not after a vanishing fraction of the space, reversing the "need ~N/2 draws" folk belief.
- closed-form anchor: exact E[T_365]=24.616586 (=1+Q(365), matching the published 24.61659), E[T_1024]=40.775954, E[T_4096]=80.880396, E[T_16384]=161.091690; C_mean=√(π/2)=1.253314, C_half=√(2 ln 2)=1.177410; m*(16384)=151 with exact P(collision by m*)=0.500101; exact ratio E[T_16384]/E[T_1024]=3.950654 (≈√16=4.0, the √N law) — both landmarks (mean and threshold) scale as √N, decisively far from the linear-N folk 16.0.

Design correction (transferable): the birthday bound — a random-ID space of N values is safe only to ~√N draws (more precisely, more-likely-than-not to collide by ~1.1774√N), NOT ~N/2 — so SIZE THE SPACE BY THE SQUARE of the expected number of draws plus a margin, grow ID/token WIDTH ~2 bits per doubling of expected usage (doubling the space buys only √2≈1.41× more safe draws; QUADRUPLE the space to halve collision risk), and read this bound into any collision-resistance (a b-bit hash resists collisions only to ~2^(b/2) queries), hash-table-load, or random-short-ID capacity plan; a plan that treats a random-ID space as "half-full is fine" silently ships a quadratic collision risk.

## VERDICT 144 mirror · 2026-07-18T11:52:41Z · to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) · P131 (+13) · APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 131 · 2026-07-18T10:37:30Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready — the single-elimination favorite-collapse (competitive-format design / order statistics / game domain): the folk model of a knockout bracket is that a bigger/longer tournament is a truer test that rewards the best player — "add more rounds and the cream rises." It is INVERTED. In a single-elimination bracket the strongest entrant must win EVERY round, so its title probability is the PRODUCT of its per-match win probabilities — a quantity that shrinks geometrically as the bracket grows. Model each entrant by a Bradley-Terry strength θ; a match between strengths a and b is won by a with probability a/(a+b). Put one favorite at strength f>1 and a field of N−1 identical "normal" entrants at strength 1; because the normals are indistinguishable the favorite draws a normal EVERY round under ANY seeding, so its per-match win probability is the constant p=f/(f+1) and its exact title probability is the closed form P_title(R)=p^R over R=log2(N) rounds — strictly decreasing in R, each added round an independent coin-weighted haircut that can only LOWER the favorite's odds. With f=3 (a 3× dominant favorite, p=0.75): R=4 (16 players)→31.6%, R=6 (64-player esports bracket)→17.8%, R=8 (256 players)→10.0% — the best player is dethroned in the large majority of runs and it gets WORSE the "truer" you make the test; seeding alone does NOT fix it (under indistinguishable normals the favorite draws a normal every round regardless of layout). Round-30 GAME-slot (competitive-format design / order statistics — a tournament-format selection-efficiency / knockout title-decay object, deliberately DISTINCT from every prior game head P099/P103 shop-reroll (optimal stopping), P107 streak-shield, P111 energy-cap, P115 matchmaking-winrate-mirage, P119 compounding-reward-inequality, P123 PRD proc compression, P127 guild-volunteer-dilemma (public goods) — the FIRST competitive-format / bracket-selection card in the game lane; rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated, P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, P130 took venture, this takes the game slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any ladder/tournament/matchmaking operator or ranking designer choosing a competitive format — the transferable correction is a single-elimination bracket does NOT reward the best player more as it grows: each knockout round is an independent coin-weighted haircut so the favorite's title probability is p^R and decays geometrically with the number of rounds; if the ladder must crown the best, don't add rounds — add DEPTH per round (best-of-N pushes each stage's win prob toward 1) or a group/Swiss stage before the bracket).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/single-elim-favorite-collapse-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE — on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=200000, FAVORITE_STRENGTH=3.0 ⇒ P_MATCH=0.75, ROUNDS=(2,4,6,8) ⇒ N=4,16,64,256, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; each trial simulates the favorite's actual R-match knockout path — win each round with constant p=f/(f+1)=0.75 against an indistinguishable normal — over TRIALS=200000, computing the three pre-registered z-gates against the closed-form p^R anchors; z on the observed proportion vs the null via Bernoulli se=√(p0(1−p0)/TRIALS), G2 via the delta method on the ratio, the P104..P132 /se convention), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo confirms the collapse: MC favorite title frequency R=2→0.562975, R=4→0.31533, R=6→0.17734, R=8→0.100455 vs the closed form 0.75^R; P(favorite loses a 64-player bracket)=0.82266 > 0.5. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1→G2→G3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 sim-correct (headline): PASS — at R=8 the Monte-Carlo favorite title frequency 0.100455 matches the closed form 0.75^8=0.1001129150390625 within z=+0.510 < SIGMA=3.0 — the simulated knockout path lands on the geometric-decay anchor.
- G2 constant geometric haircut: PASS — the MC ratio P_title(8)/P_title(4)=ratio_mc 0.3185710208353154 matches the anchor 0.75^4=0.31640625 within z=+0.916 < 3σ (delta method) — each added pair of rounds is the SAME multiplicative haircut 0.75^4, not a "truer test"; the favorite's odds decay geometrically in R.
- G3 inversion: PASS — at R=6 (64-player bracket) the MC P(favorite loses) 0.82266 > 0.5 (majority, g3_majority=True) AND matches the anchor 1−0.75^6=0.822021484375 within z=+0.747 < 3σ (g3_inversion=True) — the strongest entrant is dethroned in the large majority of runs and the folk "bigger tournament is a truer test" belief is reversed with margin.
- closed-form anchor: p=f/(f+1)=3/(3+1)=0.75; 0.75^2=0.5625, 0.75^4=0.31640625, 0.75^6=0.177978515625, 0.75^8=0.1001129150390625; R=log2(N) ⇒ N=4,16,64,256; P(favorite loses at R=6)=1−0.75^6=0.822021484375 — the favorite's title probability is p^R, a geometric decay in the number of rounds, so a 3× favorite wins a 64-player single-elim bracket only ~17.8% of the time and it gets worse with more rounds.

The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 002806188054a6eb2f768208f52629d0a3bd0d14624e0489e480efcf86c2a0f2 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P131 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P132 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) — run()/main() build a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, main() computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON — so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; TWIST — the stdout DUMP is the pretty indent=2 form, NOT the compact digest preimage (two serializations of the same dict); recompute over the compact form; the computation lives in run() (returns the results dict), so the in-process double-run calls run() twice directly; resolved against main() in the script text, not assumed. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=+0.510 / G2 z=+0.916 ratio_mc=0.318571 vs anchor 0.316406 / G3 z=+0.747 g3_majority=True g3_inversion=True P(fav loses)=0.82266); observed mc_title_freq R=2 0.562975 / R=4 0.31533 / R=6 0.17734 / R=8 0.100455, and all closed-form anchors match. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114–V143, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact — and it landed to the digit. Independently re-run in this seat's idea-engine checkout as a sanity guard: `python3 ideas/superbot-games/single_elim_favorite_collapse.py` printed `Results-JSON sha256: 002806188054…c2a0f2`, G1/G2/G3 PASS, exit 0. Design correction (transferable): a single-elimination bracket does NOT reward the best player more as it grows — each knockout round is an independent coin-weighted haircut, so the favorite's title probability is p^R and decays geometrically with the number of rounds; a 3× favorite (p=0.75) wins a 64-player bracket only ~18% of the time and it gets WORSE with more rounds. If the ladder must crown the best, don't add rounds — add DEPTH per round (best-of-N pushes each stage's win prob toward 1) or a group/Swiss stage before the bracket (averages out variance); seeding alone does NOT fix it because under indistinguishable normals the favorite draws a normal every round regardless of layout (Ryvkin & Ortmann 2008 rank single-elim the least selection-efficient common format).

## PROPOSAL 134 · 2026-07-18T12:52:35Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 147 (cohort-blended LTV understatement, round-31 VENTURE slot)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/blended-churn-ltv-understatement-2026-07-18.md
question: does collapsing a heterogeneous customer book to a single blended churn rate c̄ and computing LTV = m/c̄ UNDERSTATE the portfolio's true average LTV E[m/c] — because 1/c is convex, so Jensen forces E[m/c] ≥ m/c̄ — with the gap driven by churn DISPERSION not level (gap ≈ m·Var(c)/c̄³), reaching ~30% for churn uniform on 5%–35% (true ln(7)/0.30=6.486367 vs blended 5.000000)?

## VERDICT 147 mirror · 2026-07-18T13:19:22Z · to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) · P134 (+13) · APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 134 · 2026-07-18T12:52:35Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready — the cohort-blended LTV understatement (startup unit-economics / subscription-finance — a venture-lane pure-mechanism head): the folk model of subscriber lifetime value is that you can collapse a heterogeneous customer book to ONE blended churn rate c̄ and read the portfolio's average LTV straight off LTV = m/c̄ — "average the churn, divide the margin, done." It is NOT the average LTV: the blended number systematically UNDERSTATES the truth. Each customer's true lifetime value is m/c_i, and because 1/c is a CONVEX function of the churn rate, Jensen's inequality forces the book average E[m/c] ≥ m/E[c] = m/c̄ — the equality holds only when every customer churns at exactly the same rate. The gap is driven by churn DISPERSION, not its level (to second order gap ≈ m·Var(c)/c̄³): a book with the same mean churn but more spread has a strictly larger true LTV, because the long-lived low-churn tail contributes disproportionately (a customer at half the churn lives twice as long and is worth twice as much, so the tail is heavy on the upside). On churn uniform over 5%–35% (mean c̄=0.20, so blended LTV = m/c̄ = 1.0/0.20 = 5.000000) the TRUE average is E[m/c] = m·E[1/c] = ln(0.35/0.05)/0.30 = ln(7)/0.30 = 6.486367 — the blended figure understates the real portfolio LTV by ~30%; hold the mean fixed but tighten the band to [0.19,0.21] and the gap collapses to 0.004628, isolating dispersion (not level) as the cause. Round-31 VENTURE-slot (a subscription-finance / Jensen-convexity aggregation-bias object, deliberately DISTINCT from the prior venture heads — P102 growth cash-trough trap (cash TIMING), P130 annual-prepay financing trap (implicit-borrowing IRR/NPV), and the retention-survivorship-mirage (heterogeneity via composition drift) — this is the FIRST card isolating the CONVEXITY of LTV=m/c in the churn rate as the source of a blended-metric bias; rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated, P133 opened round-31 at the fleet slot (fan-out tail), this takes the venture slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any subscription / recurring-revenue operator or investor sizing portfolio LTV, an LTV/CAC bar, or a payback plan off a blended churn rate — the transferable correction is a heterogeneous customer book is worth MORE than its blended churn says (Jensen on the convex 1/c), so a book that looks marginal at the blended number can clear the LTV/CAC bar once the low-churn tail is counted; compute LTV per segment and AVERAGE the segment LTVs, never divide the margin by the pooled churn).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/blended-churn-ltv-understatement-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE — on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=200000, SIGMA=3.0, margin m=1.0, wide churn band [0.05,0.35] (mean c̄=0.20), narrow band [0.19,0.21] (same mean c̄=0.20, tight spread); the closed-form anchor E[m/c]=m·E[1/c]=m·ln(b/a)/(b−a) over a uniform band, blended LTV=m/c̄, and per-trial Monte-Carlo means with z on the estimated mean via se=std/√TRIALS, the P104..P132 /se convention), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo confirms the understatement: over TRIALS=200000 draws of c~Uniform[0.05,0.35] the mean of m/c lands on the convex anchor E[m/c]=6.486367 (well above the blended 5.000000), and the tight narrow band [0.19,0.21] at the same mean churn collapses the wide−narrow gap to 0.004628. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1→G2→G3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 understatement bias (headline): PASS — the WIDE-band gap E[m/c] − m/c̄ (true book average minus the blended figure) is strictly positive at z = 171.221384 ≫ SIGMA=3.0 — the blended LTV understates the true portfolio LTV, exactly as Jensen on the convex 1/c predicts.
- G2 matches the convex closed form: PASS — the Monte-Carlo mean of m/c matches the closed-form anchor E[m/c]=m·ln(b/a)/(b−a)=ln(7)/0.30=6.486367 within |z| = 0.363449 < 3σ — the true average LTV is the convex expectation, not the blended m/c̄.
- G3 dispersion-driven: PASS — the wide−narrow gap (wide-band gap minus the same-mean narrow-band gap) is positive at z = 170.572090 AND the narrow-band gap itself collapses to 0.004628 < 0.05 — holding mean churn fixed but shrinking the spread all but erases the understatement, isolating churn DISPERSION (not level) as the cause (gap ≈ m·Var(c)/c̄³).
- closed-form anchor: blended LTV = m/c̄ = 1.0/0.20 = 5.000000; true average LTV over the wide band = m·E[1/c] = ln(0.35/0.05)/0.30 = ln(7)/0.30 = 6.486367 (understatement (6.486367−5.000000)/5.000000 ≈ 29.7% ≈ 30%); wide−narrow gap dominated by Var(c) — the narrow band [0.19,0.21] at the same mean c̄=0.20 collapses the gap to 0.004628, so the second-order gap ≈ m·Var(c)/c̄³ is dispersion-driven, decisively far from the folk "blend the churn, divide" equality that holds only at zero dispersion.

NOTE the digest posture: P134 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) — main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON — so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; recompute over the compact form. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114–V145, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact — and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): a heterogeneous customer book is worth MORE than its blended churn rate says — because LTV=m/c is convex in c, Jensen forces the true portfolio average E[m/c] ≥ m/c̄ and the gap grows with churn DISPERSION (≈ m·Var(c)/c̄³), so NEVER collapse a mixed book to one blended churn and divide; compute LTV per homogeneous segment (or fit a churn-heterogeneity model) and AVERAGE the segment LTVs, and read this upward correction into any LTV/CAC bar or payback plan — a book that looks marginal at the blended number can clear the bar once the long-lived low-churn tail is counted, and conversely a blended LTV silently flatters nothing here — it UNDERSTATES, so the risk is leaving profitable acquisition on the table.

## VERDICT 146 mirror · 2026-07-18T13:39:47Z · to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) · P133 (+13) · APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 133 · 2026-07-18T12:25:02Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready — the fan-out tail amplification (fleet-ops / reliability tail-latency — a fleet-lane pure-mechanism head): the folk model of a scatter-gather is that a rare per-leaf slowdown stays rare at the request level — "each leaf misses its latency SLO only 1% of the time, so a fanned-out request is basically always fast." It is NOT: a request that fans out to N independent leaf servers and must wait for the LAST of them is slow whenever ANY leaf is slow, so its slow probability is the union P_slow(N) = 1 − (1 − p)^N, which grows toward 1 as fan-out width N rises even for a tiny per-leaf p. At p = 0.01 the MEDIAN request is already slow at only N* = ln 2 / (−ln(1 − p)) = ln 2 / (−ln 0.99) ≈ 0.693/p ≈ 69 leaves (n_star = 69, real-valued root 68.967564), and at N = 100 the request is slow 1 − 0.99^100 = 63.4% of the time — while the MEAN number of slow leaves is merely linear at N·p (just ~1 slow leaf at N=100). The tail is amplified super-linearly by fan-out width even though the average slow-leaf count is unremarkable, because "slow" is a union over leaves, not an average. Round-31 FLEET-slot (a series-reliability / union-bound tail-latency object, deliberately DISTINCT from the prior fleet heads — P121 hedged-request tail cure (the p95-deferred second copy that CURES the tail), P129 correlated-vote Condorcet ceiling, and P125 two-choices routing — this is the object the hedge cures: the all-of-N wait-for-the-last tail itself; rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated, P133 opened round-31 at the fleet slot, P134 took venture; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any fleet that fans a request across N leaves/shards/replicas and waits for all of them (scatter-gather over shards, a batch whose completion waits on its slowest task, an agent step gated on all of several downstreams) — the transferable correction is fan-out width is a tail multiplier: a 1%-per-leaf tail becomes a 63% request tail at N=100 and the median goes slow at ~69 leaves, so budget the tail against 1 − (1 − p)^N (not p), cap fan-out width, or tolerate a straggler quorum / hedge the slow few (P121) — never assume a small per-leaf tail stays small at scale).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/fan-out-tail-amplification-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE — on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=120000, P_LEAF=0.01, N_GRID=(10,69,100,200), Z_GATE=3.0; the closed-form anchors P_slow(N)=1−(1−p)^N with binomial se and mean slow leaves = N·p with se from the Binomial(N,p) per-trial variance N·p·(1−p), the P104..P134 /se convention), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo confirms the amplification: over TRIALS=120000 fan-outs the slow-request fraction lands on the union closed form at every N, the median crossover is bracketed at n_star=69, and the mean slow-leaf count stays linear. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1→G2→G3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 union-bound tail (headline): PASS — at N=100 the MC slow-request fraction 0.6335 matches the EXACT union P_slow(100)=1−0.99^100=0.633968 within |z| = 0.336 < 3σ — the whole request is slow whenever any of 100 independent leaves is slow, so a 1%-per-leaf tail is a 63.4% request tail.
- G2 median crossover: PASS — n_star=69 is the smallest N with P_slow(N)≥0.5 (bracket True: P_slow(68)<0.5≤P_slow(69); real-valued root ln2/(−ln0.99)=68.967564), AND the MC slow fraction at N*=69 (0.500142) matches the exact P_slow(69)=0.500163 within |z| = 0.015 < 3σ — the MEDIAN request goes slow at only a ~69-way fan-out, not at the naive N≈1/p=100.
- G3 mean-slow-leaves linear: PASS — at N=100 the MC mean number of slow leaves 1.000158 matches the EXACT linear anchor N·p=1.0 within |z| = 0.055 < 3σ — the AVERAGE slow-leaf count is merely linear (~1 leaf), decisively separated from the super-linear 63.4% request tail: the tail is amplified by the union over leaves, not by the mean.
- closed-form anchor: P_slow(N)=1−(1−p)^N (union / series-reliability); crossover N*=ln2/(−ln(1−p))≈0.693/p, here 68.967564→n_star=69 (bracket P_slow(68)=0.496660<0.5≤P_slow(69)=0.500163); mean slow leaves = N·p (linear); per-N exact/MC — N=10 P_slow 0.095618/0.0959 mean 0.1/0.1002, N=69 0.500163/0.500142 mean 0.69/0.688133, N=100 0.633968/0.6335 mean 1.0/1.000158, N=200 0.866020/0.866742 mean 2.0/2.003908 — the tail rises toward 1 with N while the mean stays linear.

The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 4b3de5012a6cedc99de8e446c3fdd0aa79b1988fd0594c3cbb9e33702131c42d matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P133 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132/P134 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) — main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON — so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; recompute over the compact form (the P127+ TWIST: the stdout DUMP is pretty indent=2 while the hashed payload is compact). All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=−0.336 union tail at N=100 / G2 z=−0.015 median crossover n_star=69 bracketed / G3 z=+0.055 mean slow leaves N·p=1.0), 63.4% request tail at N=100 vs ~1 mean slow leaf. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114–V147, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact — and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): a scatter-gather that waits for ALL of N leaves inherits the union tail P_slow(N)=1−(1−p)^N, so fan-out width is a tail multiplier — a 1%-per-leaf tail is a 63% request tail at N=100 and the median request goes slow at only ~69 leaves, while the mean slow-leaf count stays a harmless ~1; budget the request tail against 1−(1−p)^N not the per-leaf p, cap fan-out width, or tolerate a straggler quorum / hedge the slow few (P121's p95-deferred second copy), and never read a small per-leaf tail as a small request tail at scale.

## PROPOSAL 135 · 2026-07-18T13:44:12Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 148 (balance-triangle pick-rate inversion, round-31 GAME slot)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/balance-triangle-pick-rate-inversion-2026-07-18.md
question: in a weighted rock-paper-scissors balance triangle (skew-symmetric zero-sum game, cycle R>S>P>R with winning margins a=R>S, b=S>P, c=P>R), does the mixed-strategy Nash equilibrium play each unit in proportion to the margin of the matchup it is NOT in (x*_R:x*_P:x*_S = b:a:c), so that buffing R's margin a from the symmetric 0.2 to 0.4 moves x* from (1/3,1/3,1/3) to (R 0.25, P 0.50, S 0.25) — DROPPING R's own pick rate and DOUBLING its counter P's — while the game value stays 0 and the intuitive uniform (1/3-each) meta is exploitable by (a−c)/3?

Digest posture: WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132/P134 family — main() builds a results dict with no results_sha256 key, digests the compact json.dumps(sort_keys,compact) serialization, prints the pretty indent=2 dump + `Results-JSON sha256:`, writes no on-disk JSON). Two runs byte-identical, exit 0.

## PROPOSAL 136 · 2026-07-18T14:05:49Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 149 (base-rate PPV collapse at low prevalence, round-31 UNRELATED slot CLOSER)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/base-rate-ppv-collapse-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-31 UNRELATED-domain slot CLOSER (rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated; P133 opened round-31 at the fleet slot (fan-out tail), P134 took venture (blended-churn LTV), P135 took game (balance-triangle inversion), this CLOSES the round at the unrelated slot). Domain: probability / Bayesian inference — base-rate neglect and the collapse of positive predictive value (PPV) at low prevalence, a fleet-external pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior UNRELATED closer P100 (Kelly overbet / information theory), P104 (epidemic overshoot / SIR final size), P108 (regression to the mean / conditional-mean shrinkage), P112 (series-system MTBF collapse / reliability), P116 (arcsine lead illusion / random-walk sojourn distribution), P120 (German-tank MVUE / point estimation), P124 (Benford / leading-digit law), P128 (Stein / estimator admissibility), P132 (birthday-collision √N first-collision waiting time) — a diagnostic-POSTERIOR / PPV object, not a bet-fraction / epidemic-size / conditional-mean / MTBF / sojourn / point-estimate / leading-digit / admissibility / first-collision object. Folk belief (inverted here): "a 99%-accurate test that comes back positive means about a 99% chance you have the disease — accuracy tells me how much to trust a positive result." It is NOT: by Bayes the posterior P(disease|positive)=PPV=(sens·prev)/(sens·prev+(1−spec)·(1−prev)) depends on the base rate prev, not the accuracy alone — the intuition confuses the forward conditional sens=P(positive|disease) with its converse (base-rate neglect / prosecutor's fallacy). Mechanism: write TP=sens·prev, FP=(1−spec)(1−prev); PPV=TP/(TP+FP) is the ratio of true-positive mass (∝prev) to false-positive mass (∝1−prev), so when the disease is rare the false positives from the huge healthy majority drown the true positives. With a symmetric q-accurate test PPV=½ exactly at prev*=1−q, and for small prev PPV≈(sens/(1−spec))·prev — LINEAR in the base rate, so halving prevalence roughly halves the posterior. Pinned world: SEED=20260717, N=2,000,000 individuals per scenario, SENS=SPEC=0.99, two prevalence scenarios A prev=0.01 and B prev=0.001, SIGMA_GATE=3.0. Exact closed-form anchors (no RNG): PPV0(prev=0.01)=0.5 EXACTLY (the 50% knife-edge prev*=1−q=0.01), positive-rate p0(prev=0.01)=sens·prev+(1−spec)(1−prev)=0.0198; PPV0(prev=0.001)=0.99·0.001/(0.99·0.001+0.01·0.999)=0.0901639344…. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1→G2→G3): G1 posterior=0.5 (headline) — the MC empirical PPV at prev=0.01 matches the exact anchor 0.5 within z<3σ (Bernoulli se over the positives, se=√(PPV0(1−PPV0)/n_pos)) — a "99% accurate" positive at 1% prevalence is a coin flip, not near-certainty; G2 positive-rate — the MC empirical positive-rate at prev=0.01 matches the exact anchor 0.0198 within z<3σ (Bernoulli se over all N, se=√(p0(1−p0)/N)) — confirms the firing rate is the Bayes denominator and the tallies form the right object; G3 collapse deepens — the MC empirical PPV at prev=0.001 matches the exact anchor 0.0901639 within z<3σ (Bernoulli se over the positives) — the posterior collapses FURTHER as prevalence falls (0.5→~0.09), the base-rate mechanism made quantitative. z-scores on the /se margin: each z on an observed proportion vs the closed-form null (the P104..P132 /se convention). Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/base_rate_ppv_collapse.py (random,math,json,hashlib; computes the exact closed-form anchors, then Monte-Carlo's N=2,000,000 individuals per prevalence off the single pinned stream — diseased w.p. prev, positive w.p. sens if diseased else 1−spec — tallies TP/FP/TN/FN, forms the empirical PPV and positive-rate, evaluates the three z-gates, and emits the whole results dict — no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field; WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture — main() digests the compact json.dumps(sort_keys=True,separators=(",",":")) serialization, prints the pretty indent=2 dump + `Results-JSON sha256:`, writes no on-disk JSON; in-process double-run assertion run()×2 compact-hashed equal). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned, two runs byte-identical, exit 0): scenario A prev=0.01 TP=19766 FP=19945 n_pos=39711 empirical PPV=0.497746 positive-rate=0.019856; scenario B prev=0.001 TP=2019 FP=19816 n_pos=21835 empirical PPV=0.092466; G1 posterior=0.5 PASS (z=−0.898, PPV 0.497746 vs anchor 0.500000); G2 positive-rate PASS (z=+0.563, 0.019856 vs anchor 0.019800); G3 collapse deepens PASS (z=+1.188, PPV 0.092466 vs anchor 0.090164) — all PASS, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=89c4bd02969e51bfed210680af0d73869f93fde23149f1cc238ba77b895faac8.

Offset pinned." in this outbox) and the control/status.md baton ("next: V148 for P135 …; then round-31 UNRELATED-slot P136 closes the round"); PROPOSAL 136 → VERDICT 149 (+13). Lane CONSUMER = any operator/clinician/analyst reading a rare-event positive flag (medical screen, fraud/anomaly/intrusion alert, spam/content-moderation flag, multiple-comparison "significant" winner, any rare-class classifier): the transferable correction is "a positive result updates you from the prior prevalence to the posterior PPV=(sens·prev)/(sens·prev+(1−spec)(1−prev)), NOT to the test's accuracy — with a symmetric q-accurate test the posterior is only ½ at prevalence 1−q and collapses ~linearly in the base rate below that, so mass-screening a rare condition produces mostly false positives no matter how good the test; to make a positive mean something either raise the pre-test probability (target a higher-prevalence subgroup) or confirm with a second independent test (each independent positive multiplies the likelihood ratio sens/(1−spec) again), and always report precision (=PPV) at the DEPLOYED base rate, never the raw accuracy — this is base-rate neglect / the false-positive paradox / the prosecutor's fallacy." Adjacent to but DISTINCT from P072 pooled-screening-prevalence-wall (ideas/fleet/pooled-screening-prevalence-wall-2026-07-15.md): P072 prices test-COUNT economics (how many tests to run, wall p*=1−3^(−1/3), PERFECT tests) and named "imperfect tests (sensitivity/specificity<1)" as its FIRST out-of-scope follow-up; P136 is the complementary object — what a SINGLE positive result MEANS (posterior/PPV of one imperfect test), taking up precisely that named follow-up (same prevalence axis, orthogonal question, no shared metric). ## VERDICT 149 mirror · 2026-07-18T14:35:26Z · to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) · P136 (+13) · APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 136 · 2026-07-18T14:05:49Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready — the base-rate PPV collapse at low prevalence (probability / Bayesian inference — a fleet-external UNRELATED-lane pure-mechanism head): the folk model of a diagnostic test is that a "99%-accurate" positive result means about a 99% chance you have the disease — "accuracy tells me how much to trust a positive." It does NOT: by Bayes the posterior of one positive is P(disease|positive) = PPV = (sens·prev)/(sens·prev + (1−spec)(1−prev)), a function of the BASE RATE prev, not the accuracy alone — the intuition confuses the forward conditional sens=P(positive|disease) with its converse (base-rate neglect / the false-positive paradox / the prosecutor's fallacy). Writing TP=sens·prev and FP=(1−spec)(1−prev), PPV=TP/(TP+FP) is the ratio of true-positive mass (∝prev) to false-positive mass (∝1−prev), so when the disease is rare the false positives from the huge healthy majority drown the true positives. With a symmetric q-accurate test PPV=½ EXACTLY at prev*=1−q, and for small prev PPV≈(sens/(1−spec))·prev — LINEAR in the base rate, so halving prevalence roughly halves the posterior. At sens=spec=0.99 the posterior is EXACTLY 0.5 at prev=0.01 (the knife-edge prev*=1−q=0.01) and COLLAPSES to 0.0901639 at prev=0.001. Round-31 UNRELATED-slot CLOSER (a diagnostic-POSTERIOR / PPV object, deliberately DISTINCT from every prior UNRELATED closer — P100 Kelly overbet, P104 epidemic overshoot, P108 regression to the mean, P112 series-system MTBF, P116 arcsine lead illusion, P120 German-tank MVUE, P124 Benford, P128 Stein admissibility, P132 birthday-collision √N; rotation fleet→venture→game→unrelated, P133 opened round-31 at the fleet slot (fan-out tail), P134 took venture (blended-churn LTV), P135 took game (balance-triangle inversion), this CLOSES the round at the unrelated slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any operator/clinician/analyst reading a rare-event positive flag (medical screen, fraud/anomaly/intrusion alert, spam/content-moderation flag, multiple-comparison "significant" winner, any rare-class classifier) — the transferable correction is a positive result updates you from the prior prevalence to the posterior PPV=(sens·prev)/(sens·prev+(1−spec)(1−prev)), NOT to the test's accuracy, so mass-screening a rare condition produces mostly false positives no matter how good the test; to make a positive mean something either raise the pre-test probability (target a higher-prevalence subgroup) or confirm with an independent second test (each independent positive multiplies the likelihood ratio sens/(1−spec) again), and always report precision (=PPV) at the DEPLOYED base rate, never the raw accuracy).

ruling: APPROVE — on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N=2,000,000 individuals per scenario, sens=spec=0.99, two prevalence scenarios A prev=0.01 and B prev=0.001, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; the exact closed-form anchors PPV=(sens·prev)/(sens·prev+(1−spec)(1−prev)) and positive-rate p0=sens·prev+(1−spec)(1−prev), with z on the observed proportion vs the closed-form null via a Bernoulli standard error (se=√(PPV0(1−PPV0)/n_pos) for the posteriors, se=√(p0(1−p0)/N) for the positive-rate), the P104..P134 /se convention), a stdlib Monte-Carlo over N=2,000,000 individuals per scenario confirms the collapse: the empirical PPV at prev=0.01 lands on the exact 0.5 knife-edge, the empirical positive-rate lands on the Bayes denominator 0.0198, and the empirical PPV at prev=0.001 lands on the collapsed 0.0901639. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1→G2→G3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 posterior=0.5 (headline): PASS — the MC empirical PPV at prev=0.01 = 0.497746 matches the EXACT anchor 0.500000 within |z| = 0.898 < 3σ — a "99%-accurate" positive at 1% prevalence is a coin flip, not near-certainty; the posterior is exactly ½ at the knife-edge prev*=1−q=0.01.
- G2 positive-rate: PASS — the MC empirical positive-rate at prev=0.01 = 0.019856 matches the EXACT anchor 0.019800 within |z| = 0.563 < 3σ — the firing rate is the Bayes denominator TP+FP, confirming the tallies form the right object.
- G3 collapse deepens: PASS — the MC empirical PPV at prev=0.001 = 0.092466 matches the EXACT anchor 0.0901639 within |z| = 1.188 < 3σ — the posterior collapses FURTHER as prevalence falls (0.5→~0.09), the base-rate mechanism made quantitative: true positives (∝prev) are swamped by false positives (∝1−prev).
- closed-form anchor (no RNG): PPV(prev=0.01)=(0.99·0.01)/(0.99·0.01+0.01·0.99)=0.500000 (the 50% knife-edge prev*=1−q); positive-rate(prev=0.01)=sens·prev+(1−spec)(1−prev)=0.019800; PPV(prev=0.001)=(0.99·0.001)/(0.99·0.001+0.01·0.999)=0.0901639 — for small prev PPV≈(sens/(1−spec))·prev is LINEAR in the base rate, so the posterior scales ~with prevalence below the knife-edge, decisively far from the folk "accuracy = trust" reading that ignores the base rate entirely.

NOTE the digest posture: P136 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132/P134 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) — main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON — so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; recompute over the compact form (the P127+ TWIST: the stdout DUMP is pretty indent=2 while the hashed payload is compact).
