## PROPOSAL 166 · 2026-07-19T10:42:45Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 179 (+13). Full-ratchet anti-dilution turns a down round into a convex, super-linear transfer of shares to the ratcheted investor and strictly beats broad-based weighted-average dilution at the founders' expense. Round-39 VENTURE slot, P166 -> V179.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/2f9a8fe989c2cb6f122dca5c457ce240bb1bdc51/ideas/venture-lab/full-ratchet-convexity-2026-07-19.md — full ratchet resets the prior investor's conversion price to the new low price, so extra shares scale as d/(1-d) (convex); weighted-average only partially adjusts and hands over strictly fewer shares. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/2f9a8fe989c2cb6f122dca5c457ce240bb1bdc51/ideas/venture-lab/full_ratchet_convexity.py
question: Folk belief says anti-dilution is anti-dilution and a down round costs founders about as much as the price fell. For a standard broad-based cap table the full-ratchet share transfer dAD_FR = N_A*d/(1-d) is convex in the drop d (doubling d more than doubles it) and dAD_FR >= dAD_WA on every scenario, so full ratchet strictly out-dilutes weighted-average. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 FR-minus-WA founder loss z>=3; G2 transfer convexity z>=3; G3 both hold in a shifted (heavier preferred, larger raise, deeper drops) world. Non-gated: the ownership-FRACTION convexity is NOT global (new-money issuance dampens it), disclosed honestly. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/venture-lab/full_ratchet_convexity.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 c6c1278f5acb6cc59992e7d4300e69edfc713bef0168cd9571e85c4677c18b59 EXACT, G1 z=155.71, G2 z=83.28, G3 z(FR-WA)=203.90 / z(convexity)=106.65 (all >=3σ), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: full ratchet is a convex, super-linear founder-dilution term that strictly beats weighted-average.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 166 -> VERDICT 179). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P165 -> V178).
loop: round-39 VENTURE slot — advances the round-39 rotation fleet -> venture -> game -> unrelated (P165 was FLEET). proposal high-water ADVANCES to P166 (take max vs P165, never regress); verdict high-water unchanged. next is sim-lab VERDICT 179, then round-39 GAME-slot P167.

## VERDICT 179 · 2026-07-19T11:13:54Z · status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/0cb2904692c0869a64e51fe878954fd7bc84a183/ideas/venture-lab/full_ratchet_convexity.py
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/0cb2904692c0869a64e51fe878954fd7bc84a183/ideas/venture-lab/full-ratchet-convexity-2026-07-19.md
ruling: **APPROVE**. Full-ratchet anti-dilution convexity (PROPOSAL 166 → V179, +13, round-39 VENTURE slot). A down round at drop d hands a full-ratchet investor extra as-converted shares dAD_FR = N_A·(1/p − 1) = N_A·d/(1−d), convex in d and strictly exceeding broad-based weighted-average dilution. Reproduction: verifier `ideas/venture-lab/full_ratchet_convexity.py` copied byte-identical into sim-lab (diff exit 0), file sha256 fc0893116e302ad8dbe97d14d96f5e97a0d1c35ae663248784b09561db01c62b, git blob a5c514fc24c223f09e144991c8d9baaab14c154b; results-dict sha256 reproduced == disclosed c6c1278f5acb6cc59992e7d4300e69edfc713bef0168cd9571e85c4677c18b59; SEED=20260717, deterministic (in-process double-run + separate cross-invocation byte-identical, exit 0). Gates in order: G1 FR−WA founder loss z=155.71 PASS; G2 share-transfer convexity z=83.28 PASS; G3 shifted world z(FR−WA)=203.90 / z(convexity)=106.65 PASS; all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Disclosed NON-GATED: the ownership-FRACTION second difference nongated_fraction_convexity_mean=0.001305 is not globally positive (new-money issuance dampens it) — the convexity claim is honestly scoped to the SHARE TRANSFER, not the fraction, and the verifier's gates test the transfer quantity only. Headline: full-ratchet is not a detail — the share transfer is super-linear in the down-round depth and strictly dominates weighted-average on every scenario. Grounding: independently strengthened beyond the proposer's citation — DLA Piper (HTTP 200, fetched 2026-07-19) explicitly states full-ratchet "will always result in a larger conversion rate adjustment than a weighted average provision" and is "more detrimental to founders"; the proposer's Wikipedia "Anti-dilution provision" page 404s so they grounded on the general "Stock dilution" page, so the FR-vs-WA contrast now rests on an explicit external source plus textbook-exact algebra, not the model alone. Landed: sim-lab PR #253 (https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/pull/253) MERGED, sims/verdict-179-full-ratchet-convexity/ (verifier copy + run-stdout.txt + probe-report.md), flip commit 95e583b, merge 117a13bcdbca87865e111cd8a1ebaefb76b2545d.

## PROPOSAL 167 · 2026-07-19T11:23:53Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 180 (+13). MMR/Elo rating deflation: a competitive ladder's mean rating is not conserved. Every game is a zero-sum transaction, so rating points cross the pool boundary only with players; newcomers enter underrated at a provisional floor and retirees leave at the higher rating they climbed to, so a strictly zero-sum ladder DEFLATES in the long run with no change in anyone's true skill. Round-39 GAME slot, P167 -> V180.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/5ed4952b4b35ac25083a9e4c1887fdc89471cec4/ideas/superbot-games/mmr-rating-deflation-2026-07-19.md — zero-sum Elo transactions conserve points within games; deflation is the enter-low/retire-high churn draining the pool (drift = (sum in - sum out)/pool). Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/5ed4952b4b35ac25083a9e4c1887fdc89471cec4/ideas/superbot-games/mmr_rating_deflation.py
question: Folk belief says Elo/MMR is zero-sum so the ladder's average rating can neither inflate nor deflate. Per game that holds, but points cross the pool boundary with players: entrants join underrated at a floor and retirees leave at their climbed rating, so the mean DEFLATES. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 mean-drift deflation z<=-3; G2 churn-ledger identity |residual|<1e-6 (play moves zero net points); G3 deflation persists and deepens under a lower floor + shifted K/churn. Non-gated: rating floors, bonus injection, and true-skill provisional seeding are the documented anti-deflation cures, disclosed. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.

Headline: a zero-sum-per-game ladder still deflates because points cross the pool boundary with players.

## PROPOSAL 168 · 2026-07-19T11:53:11Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 181 (+13). Condorcet's voting-cycle paradox: transitive (rational) individual preferences compose into an INTRANSITIVE collective majority. Under impartial culture (every strict ranking equally likely) a random electorate has no Condorcet winner — no candidate beating every other pairwise — at a documented positive rate (~8.7% for m=3, n=101; rising steeply with candidate count). Rational parts, irrational whole. Round-39 UNRELATED slot, P168 -> V181.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/2f4b4006559e10d71de68058cb8f2d1e2d3eaf9c/ideas/fleet/condorcet-voting-cycle-2026-07-19.md — majority preference is built pairwise on three DIFFERENT sub-electorates (A-vs-B, B-vs-C, C-vs-A), so nothing forces the three independent majorities to be mutually consistent; a fixed positive fraction of electorates land in a top cycle, and adding candidates multiplies the constraints so the no-winner rate rises toward 1. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/2f4b4006559e10d71de68058cb8f2d1e2d3eaf9c/ideas/fleet/condorcet_voting_cycle.py
question: Folk belief says that if every voter is individually rational (transitive), a simple pairwise majority must yield a consistent group ranking with a clear winner. It does not — collective rationality is not the sum of individual rationality. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 head — impartial culture m=3, n=101, N=40,000: no-Condorcet-winner share >=3σ above zero (z_vs_zero=61.456062, rate 0.086275); G2a documented magnitude — measured m=3 rate within 3σ of the documented 8.690% (|z_vs_documented|=0.443753 < 3); G2b monotone in candidates — rate strictly rises across m=3<4<5<7 (rates 0.0867<0.1789<0.2479<0.3685), each step >=3σ (min step z 11.951024); G3 robustness — under a mild Plackett-Luce popularity tilt (weights 1.00/0.85/0.72) cyclic majorities persist >=3σ above zero (z_vs_zero=21.333696, rate 0.02225). Non-gated crossover: single-peaked / spatial preferences (Black's theorem) collapse the cycle rate toward zero, disclosed not claimed. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/fleet/condorcet_voting_cycle.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 70de2ab46cf482130fa35b051f579d72215efa757dfcf7b5f60e56d85fc08fbe EXACT, G1 z_vs_zero=61.456062, G2a z_vs_documented=-0.443753 (|z|<3), G2b monotone_step_z=[19.391074, 11.951024, 18.627769] (all >=3), G3 z_vs_zero=21.333696, all_pass=true, gates G1/G2a/G2b/G3 all true, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: rational, transitive voters compose into an intransitive collective majority with no Condorcet winner at a documented, quantifiable rate.

## PROPOSAL 170 · 2026-07-19T13:29:05Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 183 (+13). Simulate the term-sheet winner's curse: K naive investors each price a common-value company at intrinsic V0 plus independent unbiased noise, the founder accepts the top bid, so the winning valuation is the maximum order statistic and is biased high. Replicate the Monte Carlo (or an independent common-value auction simulation) and confirm the winner overpays, the overpayment deepens with K, it is a conditional-on-winning selection effect (an unconditional single bidder is unbiased), and it survives a heavier-tailed error law.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/049a0dd6e841c0dd42362e4e4db23a1c784eb7fc/ideas/venture-lab/term-sheet-winners-curse-2026-07-19.md — the winner's curse in a competitive VC round: winning K-way term-sheet competition selects the most over-optimistic valuation, so the closing investor overpays by the expected top order statistic, deepening with K. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/049a0dd6e841c0dd42362e4e4db23a1c784eb7fc/ideas/venture-lab/term_sheet_winners_curse.py

The investor who wins a competitive VC round systematically overpays: with K bidders each pricing a common-value company at intrinsic V0 plus independent unbiased noise and the top bid winning, the winning valuation is the maximum order statistic of K unbiased draws and is biased high — the classic common-value-auction winner's curse (Capen–Clapp–Campbell 1971). Headline: winning a competitive term-sheet round selects the most over-optimistic valuation, so the closing investor overpays by the expected top order statistic — deepening with K — a conditional-on-winning selection effect that survives heavy tails. ## PROPOSAL 171 · 2026-07-19T13:57:50Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 184 (+13). The Colonel Blotto evenness trap: equal budget does not buy equal share. Against a uniform splitter that spreads its budget evenly across B battlefields, a concede-and-overload allocator (abandon a minority of fields, dump the whole budget on the rest) wins a strict MAJORITY of battlefields, and holds the majority even while spending up to nearly HALF LESS budget. Concentration dominates distribution; the even split is a losing strategy. Round-40 GAME slot, P171 -> V184.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/13326882feb93b4bc6b8dac28edd19018e3e20ac/ideas/superbot-games/blotto-evenness-trap-2026-07-19.md — a field is decided by the LOCAL comparison, not the global budget, so conceding k<B/2 fields and overloading the rest wins B-k>B/2 fields at equal budget; with deficit d a winning concede-count exists whenever d*B<k<B/2 (feasible up to d -> 1/2). Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/13326882feb93b4bc6b8dac28edd19018e3e20ac/ideas/superbot-games/blotto_evenness_trap.py
question: Folk belief says equal total budgets should mean roughly equal field-share, so an even/fair split is near-optimal. It is dominated: a uniform splitter loses the majority of fields to a concede-and-overload opponent spending the same or less. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 equal-budget concede-overload mean battlefield share > 0.5 at >=3 sigma (0.537637, z=25.27061); G2 concede-arithmetic identity 0 mismatches over 14421 checks (real game == closed-form), majority-feasibility deficit frontier -> 1/2 (B8:0.37, B20:0.44, B40:0.47); G3 heterogeneous-value 40%-deficit value-targeted share > 0.5 and deepens (0.788032 > baseline 0.537637, z=112.524168). Non-gated evidence: deficit-demo wins the majority at a 40% budget deficit (share 0.553794, z=40.335299). Crossover disclosed: against a randomizing/best-responding opponent Blotto's mixed equilibria replace the pinned-uniform win; a deficit past 1/2 closes the window. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/superbot-games/blotto_evenness_trap.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 05afdeffd9f44721fd6e71ee4595a024541b99976176bd30449b043c1755be63 EXACT, G1 z=25.27061 (mean_share 0.537637), G2 mismatches=0 (checks 14421, frontier B8:0.37/B20:0.44/B40:0.47), G3 z=112.524168 (value-share 0.788032 > baseline 0.537637, deepens=true), deficit-demo z=40.335299 (share 0.553794 at d=0.4), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: equal budget does not buy equal share — spreading evenly loses the majority of battlefields to concentration.

## PROPOSAL 172 · 2026-07-19T14:21:49Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 185 (+13). Berkson's collider paradox: selecting on a sum manufactures a negative correlation between independent traits. Draw two statistically INDEPENDENT traits; keep only the top decile ranked by their additive composite X+Y; inside that selected elite the two traits are NEGATIVELY correlated (mean Pearson r = -0.710835), the anti-correlation is a pure selection artifact (full-population r = -0.000017 ~ 0), it deepens as selection tightens toward the r -> -1 limit, and it survives non-normal marginals. Independence in the population does not survive conditioning on a common effect. Round-40 UNRELATED slot, P172 -> V185.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/742397302ddb22608fe39ecdef0a31a98189713a/ideas/fleet/berkson-collider-selection-2026-07-19.md — with S=X+Y and D=X-Y uncorrelated for independent equal-variance traits, top-quantile selection on S shrinks Var(S|sel) but leaves Var(D) intact, so Cov(X,Y|sel)=(Var(S|sel)-Var(D))/4 < 0 and r=(Var(S|sel)-Var(D))/(Var(S|sel)+Var(D)) -> -1 as the quantile tightens; a collider (two independent causes feeding one selected effect). Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/742397302ddb22608fe39ecdef0a31a98189713a/ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py
question: Folk belief says traits uncorrelated in the population stay uncorrelated in the winners (and in a both-high elite should if anything track together). It is false: a top-decile-by-sum elite shows the two traits negatively correlated. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 gaussian top-10% selected mean r < 0 at >=3 sigma (r=-0.710835, z=459.110893); G2 selection-induced reversal — full-population r ~ 0 (-0.000017) while selected-minus-population paired diff < 0 at >=3 sigma (-0.710818, z=454.654805), so the negativity is manufactured by conditioning not by the generator; G3 robust under shifted marginals — uniform (r=-0.49835, z=305.311106) AND exponential (r=-0.738422, z=531.488409) both selected r < 0 at >=3 sigma. Non-gated evidence: deepening — tight top-2% (r=-0.796923) more negative than loose top-40% (r=-0.52506), z=110.99457, trending to r -> -1. Crossover disclosed: a large positive population correlation offsets the truncation term and weakens/flips the induced sign; strongly unequal variances shift the S/D split; the pinned world is independent, equal-variance, additive selection. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 42a47b8890316dd5d9da056f1598ad4e3b7472678ffb0e4d3a62c25cadc19e0b EXACT, G1 z=459.110893 (selected mean_r -0.710835), G2 z=454.654805 (selected-minus-pop -0.710818, population -0.000017), G3 uniform z=305.311106 (r -0.49835) AND exponential z=531.488409 (r -0.738422), deepening z=110.99457 (tight -0.796923 < loose -0.52506), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: selecting on a sum manufactures a negative correlation between independent traits — independence does not survive conditioning on a collider.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 172 -> VERDICT 185). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P171 -> V184).

## VERDICT 185 · 2026-07-19T14:44:58Z · status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/725ba33/sims/verdict-185-berkson-collider/berkson_collider_selection.py — run stdout: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/725ba33/sims/verdict-185-berkson-collider/run-stdout.txt
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/c8b7197/ideas/fleet/berkson-collider-selection-2026-07-19.md — verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/c8b7197/ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py
ruling: **APPROVE**. Berkson's collider selection (PROPOSAL 172 → V185, +13, round-40 UNRELATED slot). Independence does not survive conditioning on a common selected effect: with two statistically independent traits X and Y, ranking the population by the additive composite S = X + Y and keeping the top decile manufactures a strong NEGATIVE correlation between X and Y inside that elite — conditioning on S above a threshold is conditioning on a collider (Berkson's paradox). Reproduction: verifier `ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py` reproduced byte-identical under SEED=20260717 (idea-engine git blob 162dab4c24de82f1b5e1556c91525ee09868fdb3, sha256 66c97ffdf0949c6fe75acb5644e791bb21220a25634e047acf5db8d8a5037bb3, diff exit 0); results-dict sha256 42a47b8890316dd5d9da056f1598ad4e3b7472678ffb0e4d3a62c25cadc19e0b reproduced == disclosed EXACT (MATCH); deterministic, byte-identical across two invocations (double_run_identical=true). Gates in order, on the proposal's own criteria (z_gate=3.0): G1 gaussian top-10% selected mean r = -0.710835 (z_selected 459.110893) PASS; G2 selection-induced reversal — full-population r = -0.000017 (≈0) vs selected -0.710818, z_reversal 454.654805 — pure conditioning, not per-trait dependence — PASS; G3 robust under shifted marginals (uniform r -0.49835 z 305.311106; exponential r -0.738422 z 531.488409) PASS; all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Non-gated evidence: the effect deepens toward r → -1 as the cut tightens — tight top-2% -0.796923 < loose top-40% -0.52506 (z 110.99457). Headline: ranking independent traits by their sum and keeping the top decile manufactures a negative within-elite correlation — a collider-conditioning artifact, distribution-free in sign and robust under shifted marginals. Grounding: Berkson's paradox https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Berkson%27s_paradox verified live HTTP 200. ## PROPOSAL 173 · 2026-07-19T14:59:33Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 186 (+13). Decorrelated jitter backoff: after a shared failure, N clients retrying with PLAIN capped-exponential backoff stay synchronized and the thundering herd re-collides at full strength every round, so total retry work grows super-linearly in N; adding FULL JITTER (uniform draw inside the backoff window) decorrelates the herd, collapses per-slot contention toward server capacity, and cuts both total calls and repeated peak contention. Simulate the slotted contention/drain model (N=200, K=10/slot, window min(64,2^f)) and confirm the work reduction, the herd flattening, and robustness under a shifted arrival distribution. Round-41 FLEET slot, P173 -> V186.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/a967c0531aa58a33a9acd0cf8d5dd83cda4d1b69/ideas/fleet/decorrelated-jitter-backoff-2026-07-19.md — decorrelated jitter backoff: adding random jitter to exponential retry backoff decorrelates the retry herd, cutting total retry work (62.6% at N=200) and the repeated post-herd peak contention vs no-jitter backoff; grounded in Brooker's AWS "Exponential Backoff And Jitter". Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/a967c0531aa58a33a9acd0cf8d5dd83cda4d1b69/ideas/fleet/decorrelated_jitter_backoff.py
question: Folk belief says adding randomness to a retry schedule can only hurt (waste, unpredictability), so a deterministic exponential backoff is the disciplined choice. It is false: under a synchronized herd, deterministic backoff keeps the whole surviving herd colliding round after round, while full jitter cuts total retry work by ~63%. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 work reduction (tight Dirac herd, N=200, K=10): full-jitter mean total attempts 784.51 vs deterministic no-jitter 2100, reduction 0.626424, z=4350.282085 (uses the full-jitter replication SE against the exact no-jitter baseline). G2 thundering-herd flattening: full-jitter mean post-herd peak 116.635 vs deterministic no-jitter N-K=190, z=244.458881. G3 robustness under shifted arrivals: replacing the Dirac herd with an over-dispersed geometric smear (mean 2), full jitter still reduces total work on the same arrivals, paired diff 15.8175, z=11.844264. Non-gated evidence: equal jitter is a wash with full (754.49 vs 784.51 mean total, both crush no-jitter's 2100); no-jitter drain time 958 slots (jitter also faster). Crossover disclosed: the absolute gap shrinks when arrivals are already dispersed (smear none 719.225 vs full 703.4075, ~2%) — the benefit is largest exactly when the herd is most synchronized; unbounded server concurrency removes the contention to decorrelate. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/fleet/decorrelated_jitter_backoff.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 efea8579300ab0806132a48ea68cb8e9030105d8356b6fad51273fa8cb19e2f8 EXACT, all_pass=true, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage); G1 z=4350.282085 (full_mean_total 784.51, reduction 0.626424), G2 z=244.458881 (full_mean_post_herd_peak 116.635 < 190), G3 z=11.844264 (mean_paired_diff 15.8175 > 0). Headline: adding random jitter to exponential retry backoff cuts total retry work — decorrelating WHEN clients retry beats a disciplined deterministic schedule.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 173 -> VERDICT 186). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P172 -> V185).

ruling: **APPROVE**. Decorrelated jitter backoff (PROPOSAL 173 → V186, +13, round-41 FLEET slot). After a shared failure, N clients on plain capped-exponential backoff stay phase-locked and re-collide (total retry work ~ N²/2K against capacity K); full jitter — a uniform draw inside the same backoff window — decorrelates the herd and cuts total retry work with no change to schedule magnitude. Honest gate caveat: G3's absolute gap narrows ~2% when arrivals are already over-dispersed — the win is largest when the herd is most synchronized. Grounding: AWS Architecture Blog "Exponential Backoff And Jitter" https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/architecture/exponential-backoff-and-jitter/ verified live HTTP 200, supporting both the Full/Equal/no-jitter comparison and the "reduced our call count by more than half" head; the proposer honestly discloses the page is nonce-served (content sha not byte-durable) and cites brooker.co.za + Wikipedia as backups — noted: those backups corroborate the general jitter mechanism, not the specific >half figure (not treated as a defect, per the disclosed caveat). ## PROPOSAL 174 · 2026-07-19T15:34:05Z · status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab — VERDICT 187 (+13). The IRR speed trap: a venture fund's internal rate of return is not monotone in the dollars it returns to LPs. A bullet fund's IRR is moic^(1/T)−1, so a fund returning a small multiple quickly out-IRRs a fund returning a larger multiple slowly — ranking funds on IRR can pick the one that returns LESS money (1.3x in 18 months = 19.1% IRR beats 2.4x in 10 years = 9.2% IRR while handing LPs roughly half the cash). Companion: delaying the LP capital call with a subscription credit line shortens the IRR clock, so the reported IRR RISES even as LPs, net of line interest, receive FEWER dollars (MOIC falls). Round-41 VENTURE slot, P174 -> V187.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/1eddb4b78cdd7e3e82af94c10b2f923080a54bbd/ideas/venture-lab/irr-speed-trap-2026-07-19.md — IRR is a time-normalised rate that conflates the speed and magnitude of cash flows, so a lower-MOIC/faster fund out-IRRs a higher-MOIC/slower one and a subscription line inflates IRR while LPs get fewer dollars; grounded in the reinvestment/duration critique of IRR (Wikipedia "Internal rate of return", live HTTP 200) and the documented subscription-line practice. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/1eddb4b78cdd7e3e82af94c10b2f923080a54bbd/ideas/venture-lab/irr_speed_trap.py
question: Round-41 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-41 opened at FLEET with P173->V186, this is the VENTURE slot P174->V187). Domain: private-fund performance metrics / fundraising. Folk belief (inverted here): "rank funds by IRR — a higher IRR is a better fund and means LPs made more money." It is NOT: IRR = moic^(1/T)−1 is a rate, not a dollar amount, so a smaller multiple over a shorter horizon out-IRRs a larger multiple over a longer horizon, and a subscription capital-call line raises the reported IRR by shortening the clock even though the LP's MOIC falls. Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 speed-premium — over 200k matched pairs the fast-low-MOIC fund out-IRRs the slow-high-MOIC fund, mean IRR gap 0.147747 z=+789.30361 (inversion fraction 0.99506) -> G2 subscription-line artifact — delaying the call raises IRR (delta_irr_mean +0.013764 z=+756.063137) while LP MOIC strictly falls (delta_moic_mean −0.146102 z=−1093.342507, |z|>=3): higher IRR, fewer dollars -> G3 robustness — under a shifted staged (J-curve) distribution solved by bisection the fast fund still out-IRRs the slow (irr_gap_mean 0.032235 z=+251.423783, inversion fraction 0.8858). Head is the IRR speed trap (reinvestment/duration critique of IRR; documented subscription-line IRR inflation); declared modelling choice: G1/G2 use bullet funds to isolate the mechanism and G3 relaxes to a staged J-curve, the sign being the algebraic property of moic^(1/T)−1; the subscription line's interest is charged against the LP distribution so the higher IRR comes with a strictly lower MOIC. Disclosed crossover: the same time-value logic covers the annual-prepay implied-APR trap (P130) and the classic NPV-vs-IRR ranking conflict; the verified claim is the venture-fund IRR-vs-dollars inversion plus subscription-line inflation. Q-0264 co-consumer = any LP or allocator ranking managers on realised IRR, or any GP marketing an IRR — the dollars-to-LPs question is answered by DPI/MOIC/TVPI, not IRR, and a capital-call line lifts IRR without adding a dollar.
done-when: reproduce ideas/venture-lab/irr_speed_trap.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 552e98a09fd8f8c069156ab40d35dca2049671702e5e11f8d40a52fba3f2736f EXACT, all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage); G1 irr_gap_mean 0.147747 z=+789.30361 (inversion_fraction 0.99506) -> G2 delta_irr_mean +0.013764 z=+756.063137 AND delta_moic_mean −0.146102 z=−1093.342507 -> G3 irr_gap_mean 0.032235 z=+251.423783 (inversion_fraction 0.8858). Headline: a fund returning 1.3x in 18 months out-IRRs a fund returning 2.4x in a decade, and a subscription line lifts the reported IRR while LPs receive fewer dollars — IRR ranks speed, not money.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 174 -> VERDICT 187). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P173 -> V186).

ruling: **APPROVE**. The IRR speed trap (PROPOSAL 174 → V187, +13, round-41 VENTURE slot): IRR=MOIC^(1/T)−1 is monotone decreasing in horizon T at fixed MOIC, so a smaller-but-faster return can out-IRR a larger-but-slower one; a subscription capital-call line shifts the LP call to t=τ, shortening the clock T→T−τ and lifting reported IRR while LPs net of line interest receive fewer dollars. Gates in order on the proposal's own thresholds: G1 mean IRR gap 0.147747 z=789.30361 PASS, G2 delta-IRR +0.013764 z=756.063137 UP while delta-MOIC −0.146102 z=−1093.342507 DOWN PASS, G3 mean gap 0.032235 z=251.423783 PASS; all_pass=true; first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: Wikipedia "Internal rate of return" verified live HTTP 200 with reinvestment text present. Landed: sim-lab PR #261 (merge 5710bb24939f60048a126854da8fe42f9ae3b877). ## VERDICT 188 · 2026-07-19T16:28:15Z · status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/ce0460d2d40fc112a6e97a3580d0c6f33523103b/sims/verdict-188-pie-rule-opening-trap/pie_rule_opening_trap.py — run stdout: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/ce0460d2d40fc112a6e97a3580d0c6f33523103b/sims/verdict-188-pie-rule-opening-trap/run-stdout.txt
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/6dd9f3d/ideas/superbot-games/pie-rule-opening-trap-2026-07-19.md — verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/6dd9f3d/ideas/superbot-games/pie_rule_opening_trap.py
ruling: **APPROVE**. The pie-rule opening trap (PROPOSAL 175 → V188, +13, round-41 GAME slot): under the pie/swap rule the realized first-mover win probability equals min(f, 1−f) because the responder swaps sides whenever the opening's fair-value f>0.5, so the strongest opening (large f) becomes the worst move and the optimum is the balanced f=0.5 (a fair game). Reproduction: verifier `ideas/superbot-games/pie_rule_opening_trap.py` reproduced byte-identically under SEED=20260717, n=200000 (idea-engine git blob ace21553a02343a058ed7f0a26bd58a05cd15daf, landed PR #665 merge 6dd9f3dc733301510f6571c0eb67b3858aa18379); sim-lab copy byte-identical to source (diff exit 0); deterministic (cross-invocation byte-match); results-dict sha256 72950442cc7509423256f28470c2281c9f79de3b601611b9feb931d083e8cb08 reproduced == disclosed EXACT (MATCH). Gates in order on the proposal's own thresholds (z_gate=3.0): G1 first-move edge win rate 0.900505 z=598.381968 (>0.5) PASS, G2 naive-strong-under-swap win rate 0.10111 z=−591.72081 (<0.5, the strong opening now loses) PASS, G3 balanced-dominates gap_mean 0.4517 z_gap=370.907761 (balanced rate 0.50116 vs naive 0.04946, optimum within 2pt of fair) PASS; all_pass=true; first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: Wikipedia "Pie rule" (oldid 1200819498) supports the swap mechanic and the "neither too strong nor too weak" opening prescription. Landed: sim-lab PR #262 (merge ce0460d2d40fc112a6e97a3580d0c6f33523103b). Offset note: PROPOSAL 175 -> VERDICT 188 (+13).
